net_income: $3.86 billion (applicable to common shareholders), up 38.8% YoY
key_metrics: Provision for credit losses: $339 million (down 14.6% YoY)
qoq_changes: Total assets increased to $1.81 trillion from $1.68 trillion at year-end 2024
Total deposits grew to $490.2 billion from $433.0 billion at year-end 2024
new_risks: Elevated focus on cybersecurity and model risk management as outlined in the MD&A index
Ongoing credit risk exposure, though provision for credit losses decreased YoY to $339 million
management_tone: Bullish, driven by strong double-digit YoY growth in net revenues and a significant rebound in investment banking activity.
analyst_note: Goldman Sachs delivered a robust Q3 2025, highlighted by a nearly 20% increase in net revenues and a 39% surge in net earnings applicable to common shareholders. The exceptional performance in investment banking and investment management more than offset a slight YoY decline in market making revenues.
